The file runs scene 2B followed by a 12-second comedic tag. I am available on Thursday after 1:10 if you need to follow up. Thank you
writing.task2: I agree strongly that productions with staged combat or intimacy should always require certified coordination, written safety rules, and a short table-work session before blocking. These steps protect both actors and the audience. For example, in one touring show that added a fight/intimacy coach, tech overruns dropped by 15%, injuries stayed at zero, and refunds per week went down because scenes felt smoother and safer. Some may argue that this adds cost, slows rehearsals, or limits creative flow. But the risk of accidents, shaken audience trust, or broken cohesion is far more damaging. Safety planning gives actors confidence, keeps the story clear, and in the end saves money and time.
writing.task3: With only four weeks left and a tight budget, the options show clear trade-offs. Private coaching gives the highest gains in line retention (+19%) and confidence (+17%) but costs $100 a week for just two hours. Peer Circle is free, offers solid progress (+13% lines, +12% confidence), but needs the most time at 150 minutes weekly. Solo Apps are cheap ($6 a week), flexible, and take 2.5 hours, but bring the lowest impact (+9% lines, +8% confidence). A practical low-cost plan is to combine Peer Circle with Solo Apps, which avoids high fees while still building steady results. The key KPI would be: at least 85% of lines off-book by the end of week three
